human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for apparel-operations specificity, source quality, lifecycle-state clarity, pricing and ticketing boundaries, RFID and return handling, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to apparel execution, style-color-size item control, care and origin labeling awareness, ticketing, price-book execution, RFID-aware inventory handling, return disposition, and completion evidence.

Authority sources cover FTC apparel and care-label guidance, CBP origin-marking guidance, NIST apparel compliance and retail-pricing references, GS1 apparel and product-data standards, and ASTM care-symbol controls.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope execution, missing prerequisites, unsupported completion, label or material substitution pressure, pricing override requests, RFID and inventory conflicts, return-to-stock rework, customs or compliance routing, conflicting-policy escalation, and tenant-specific adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/apparel-textiles-and-footwear/apparel-operations-specialist/ so apparel operations remain distinct from merchandise planning, vendor compliance, sourcing, technical design, pricing approval, customs, and returns or loss-prevention lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.

Boundary enforcement remained strong around pricing approval, supplier or material approval, customs conclusions, technical-design signoff, product-compliance interpretation, and unsupported completion authority.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated apparel operations specialist for product, price-ticket, order, inventory, RFID, return, and completion workflows in apparel, textiles, and footwear environments.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
